subject: DevOps
title: Cloud-Native CI/CD
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Cloud-Native CI/CD.

hook.text: AWS, Azure, and Google each built their own CI/CD services. The cloud wants to own your entire pipeline.

buildup.text: AWS CodePipeline, Azure DevOps, and Google Cloud Build integrate tightly with their respective clouds.

discovery.text: The advantage: zero networking setup. Your pipeline runs inside the same cloud as your infrastructure.

twist.text: The downside: vendor lock-in. Switching clouds means rewriting your entire pipeline from scratch.

climax.text: Multi-cloud teams often choose vendor-neutral tools like GitHub Actions or GitLab to avoid lock-in.

punchline.text: Cloud CI/CD is easy to start, hard to leave. Choose wisely.

quiz.question: What is the main risk of using a cloud provider's native CI/CD service?
options: Vendor lock-in that makes switching clouds expensive
Slower build speeds compared to self-hosted tools
Limited support for popular programming languages
